description: A Korean rice dish topped with assorted seasoned vegetables, gochujang, and commonly beef and egg. The name literally refers to mixed rice, and the components are mixed together before eating.
preparation_steps: Prepare individual toppings such as sautéed or blanched vegetables and any protein.
Arrange the toppings separately over the rice.
Add gochujang and sesame oil, with egg if using.

nutrition.protein: Moderate; higher with meat, egg, or tofu

health_advice.benefits: Can be well-balanced because it combines grains, vegetables, and protein in one bowl.
health_advice.risks: Sodium may be high if large amounts of gochujang, soy sauce, or pickled sides are used.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use a variety of vegetables, lean protein, and a moderate amount of sauce.

dietary_notes.note: Vegetarian and vegan versions are common. Gluten-free suitability depends on the gochujang and soy-based condiments used.

allergens: Possible gluten in some gochujang or soy sauce formulations
common_variations: Dolsot bibimbap in a hot stone bowl
